chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI CP No.S-1413 of 2025 __________________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature(s) of Judge(s) 1. For orders on office objection Nos.1 to 4 a/w reply as at A. 2. For hearing of CMA No.8960/2025 3. For hearing of main case. 23.12.2025. Mr. Qamar Abbas, advocate for the petitioner Mr. Pervaiz Ahmed Mastoi, AAG a/w SI Shahid Mustafa, Crime Branch Invest. Sindh Karachi and Anees Ahmed, SHO, Peerabad District West Karachi --------- Through this petition, the petitioner claims following relief(s):- “A) TO DIRECT the officials Respondents to act strictly in accordance with law and to entertain the Petitioner's application dated 08/12/2025, strictly in accordance with law; B) TO DIRECT the inspector General of police Sindh and other relevant officials to ensure full protection and security of the petitioner from any kind of harassment, threats, or coercion from the hands of Respondent No.08 to Respondent No.23 and to take strict departmental action against; C) RESTRAIN the Respondents and his subordinates from harassing, threatening, arresting, raiding, or taking any coercive or Illegal action against the petitioner or her family and directed the Respondent No.13 no fake/fabricated FIR shall be lodged against the petitioner or her family members without due course of law; D) Any other relief or relief(s) which this Honourable Court, may deem, fit and proper under the circumstances of the Petition.” Counsel for the petitioner submits that there was a business deal with the son of the petitioner and Anees Ahmed, SHO, Peerabad District West Karachi and in order to usurp the amount payable by Anees Ahmed, SHO, Peerabad, FIR No.591/2025 u/s. 9(i)(e) of the Sindh Control of Narcotic Substance Act, 2024, registered at PS Peerabad District West Karachi and huge quantity of Charas was foisted upon the son of the petitioner.

Pervaiz Ahmed Mastoi, AAG a/w SI Shahid Mustafa, Crime Branch Invest. Sindh Karachi and Anees Ahmed, SHO, Peerabad District West Karachi --------- Through this petition, the petitioner claims following relief(s):- “A) TO DIRECT the officials Respondents to act strictly in accordance with law and to entertain the Petitioner's application dated 08/12/2025, strictly in accordance with law; B) TO DIRECT the inspector General of police Sindh and other relevant officials to ensure full protection and security of the petitioner from any kind of harassment, threats, or coercion from the hands of Respondent No.08 to Respondent No.23 and to take strict departmental action against; C) RESTRAIN the Respondents and his subordinates from harassing, threatening, arresting, raiding, or taking any coercive or Illegal action against the petitioner or her family and directed the Respondent No.13 no fake/fabricated FIR shall be lodged against the petitioner or her family members without due course of law; D) Any other relief or relief(s) which this Honourable Court, may deem, fit and proper under the circumstances of the Petition.” Counsel for the petitioner submits that there was a business deal with the son of the petitioner and Anees Ahmed, SHO, Peerabad District West Karachi and in order to usurp the amount payable by Anees Ahmed, SHO, Peerabad, FIR No.591/2025 u/s. 9(i)(e) of the Sindh Control of Narcotic Substance Act, 2024, registered at PS Peerabad District West Karachi and huge quantity of Charas was foisted upon the son of the petitioner. He further submits that for the proper inquiry in the matter, the petitioner has filed an application dated 08.12.2025 with the IGP Sindh and Additional IGP Sindh but no action on the said application has been taken. It appears from the reply filed by the respondents No.4, 9 and Anees Ahmed, SHO, Peerabad District West Karachi that the family of the petitioner was involved in drug trafficking and in order to counterblast FIR No.591/2025 registered against the petitioner’s son, present petition has been filed to deter the police officials from recording the evidence before the learned trial Court. Learned AAG submits that the petitioner was involved in heinous crime. There is no ill-will of SHO Anees Ahmed against the petitioner’s son. The demand draft so placed on record is a managed document and SHO Anees Ahmed has got no concern with the alleged business deal. He further submits that it may be a valid defence for the petitioner before the learned trial Court regarding her son’s involvement in the narcotics case but cannot be considered at this stage, any action in that regard will amount to harassment against witnesses. Be that as it may, since the petitioner’s son has been booked in crime and the petitioner alleges that he has been falsely booked. The petitioner is at liberty to avail her remedy before the learned trial Court in accordance with the law by filing appropriate application. The official respondents are directed to ensure that no harassment is caused to the petitioner and if petitioner approaches learned trial Court or any other Court, she shall be dealt in accordance with law.

The petitioner is at liberty to avail her remedy before the learned trial Court in accordance with the law by filing appropriate application. The official respondents are directed to ensure that no harassment is caused to the petitioner and if petitioner approaches learned trial Court or any other Court, she shall be dealt in accordance with law. This petition stands disposed of in the above terms alongwith pending application(s). A copy of this order shall be sent to SSP concerned for compliance. JUDGE Nadir*
